description: HP 8116A Pulse/Function Generator (1 mHz to 50 MHz), fully HP-IB programmable. Uses HP's front-panel mnemonic program code set (e.g. FRQ frequency, AMP amplitude, OFS offset, DTY duty cycle, WID pulse width). Operating modes M1-M8, control modes CT0-CT4, waveform W0-W4, trigger slope T0-T2. Program codes are the same as front-panel mnemonics; lower-case may replace any/all capitals. Parameters with units must be terminated by their delimiter (e.g. 'BUR 17 #'). The 8116A does not necessarily process codes in the order sent: universal messages (mode, waveform, trigger) are processed before parameter data. Sweep functions (STA/STP/MRK/SWT, modes M5-M8) require Option 001. Output messages terminate with CR/LF or EOI. Note: in the source manual OCR, the frequency mnemonic FRQ is often rendered as 'FRO' (O/Q confusion); the documented mnemonic is FRQ.

identity.description: Learn String. When addressed to talk, returns an 89-character (161 for Opt. 001) ASCII string of the current mode/parameter settings, e.g. starting 'M1 ,CT0,T1 ,W1 ,H0,A0,L0,C0,D1 ,BUR...'. The 8116A has no IDN-style identity query; CST is the only learn/identify output.

commands.name: operating_mode
commands.description: Selects the operating mode: M1=NORM, M2=TRIG, M3=GATE, M4=E.WID (external width), M5=I.SWP (internal sweep, Opt.001), M6=E.SWP (external sweep, Opt.001), M7=I.BUR (internal burst, Opt.001), M8=E.BUR (external burst, Opt.001).

commands.name: control_mode
commands.description: Selects the control (modulation) mode: CT0=off, CT1=FM, CT2=AM, CT3=PWM, CT4=VCO.

commands.name: trigger_slope
commands.description: Selects the external trigger slope: T0=off, T1=positive slope, T2=negative slope.

commands.name: haversine
commands.description: Haversine (-90 degrees) selection: H0=off, H1=on.

commands.name: waveform
commands.description: Selects the output waveform: W0=off (dc), W1=sine, W2=triangle, W3=square, W4=pulse.

commands.name: frequency
commands.description: Sets the output frequency. Interrogate the current setting with 'IFRQ' (talker returns e.g. 'FRQ 1.00 Hz'). Parameter string must be terminated with a unit delimiter.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: 1.00 mHz to 50.0 MHz (3 digit resolution); MZ=millihertz, HZ=hertz, KHZ=kilohertz, MHZ=megahertz

commands.name: duty_cycle
commands.description: Sets the duty cycle (sine, triangle, square). Interrogate with 'IDTY'. Range 10%-90% for 1 mHz to 999 kHz; 20%-80% for 1 MHz to 9.99 MHz; must be 50% for frequencies >= 10 MHz.

commands.name: pulse_width
commands.description: Sets the pulse width (pulse mode / E.WID mode). Interrogate with 'IWID'.

parameters.name: width
parameters.range: 10.0 ns to 999 ms (3 digit resolution); NS=nanoseconds, US=microseconds, MS=milliseconds

commands.name: amplitude
commands.description: Sets the output amplitude (peak-to-peak). Interrogate with 'IAMP'. AMP/OFS are active when HIL/LOL are not.

parameters.name: amplitude
parameters.range: 10.0 mVpp to 16.0 Vpp into 50 ohm (up to 32 Vpp into high impedance); MV=millivolts, V=volts

commands.name: offset
commands.description: Sets the DC offset. Interrogate with 'IOFS'.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.range: 0 to +/- 7.95 V (0 to +/- 795 mV on low level window); MV=millivolts, V=volts

commands.name: high_level
commands.description: Sets the output high level. Interrogate with 'IHIL'. HIL/LOL are active when AMP/OFS are not. When changing both HIL and LOL, reprogram both in the same string to avoid an error condition.

commands.name: low_level
commands.description: Sets the output low level. Interrogate with 'ILOL'. When changing both HIL and LOL, reprogram both in the same string to avoid an error condition.

commands.name: burst_number
commands.description: Sets the burst number (number of cycles per burst, internal burst mode). Interrogate with 'IBUR'. Must be terminated with the '#' delimiter.

commands.name: repetition_rate
commands.description: Sets the repetition rate (time between internal burst starts). Interrogate with 'IRPT'.

commands.name: sweep_start_frequency
commands.description: Sets the sweep start frequency (Opt. 001). Interrogate with 'ISTA'.

commands.name: sweep_stop_frequency
commands.description: Sets the sweep stop frequency (Opt. 001). Interrogate with 'ISTP'.

commands.name: sweep_marker_frequency
commands.description: Sets the sweep marker frequency (Opt. 001). Interrogate with 'IMRK'.

commands.name: sweep_time
commands.description: Sets the sweep time (Opt. 001). Interrogate with 'ISWT'.

commands.name: limit
commands.description: Limit function: L0=off, L1=on.

commands.name: complement
commands.description: Complement output: C0=off (normal output), C1=on (complemented output).

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Output disable: D0=off (output enabled), D1=on (output disabled).

commands.name: autovernier_mode
commands.description: Autovernier mode: A0=off, A1=on. The autovernier function MUST be enabled (A1) before any digit increment/decrement (MU/SU/LU/MD/SD/LD) can be executed.

commands.name: autovernier_msd_up
commands.description: Autovernier: increment most significant digit up. Requires A1 first.

commands.name: autovernier_2nd_up
commands.description: Autovernier: increment second significant digit up. Requires A1 first.

commands.name: autovernier_lsd_up
commands.description: Autovernier: increment least significant digit up. Requires A1 first.

commands.name: autovernier_msd_down
commands.description: Autovernier: decrement most significant digit down. Requires A1 first.

commands.name: autovernier_2nd_down
commands.description: Autovernier: decrement second significant digit down. Requires A1 first.

commands.name: autovernier_lsd_down
commands.description: Autovernier: decrement least significant digit down. Requires A1 first.

commands.name: service_request_mask
commands.description: Service Request control for timing errors: SR1 suppresses the Service Request (SRQ) for a TIMING ERROR (status byte bit 1 still sets); SR0 re-activates the service request for timing errors. Stored ROM settings have SR set to 0.

commands.name: execute_self_test
commands.description: Initiates the RAM/hardware self-test routine. On fault detection the 8116A sets bits 4 (System Failure) and 7 (Service Request) of its HP-IB status byte. Read result via serial poll.

commands.name: learn_string
commands.description: Learn String (talker function). When addressed to talk after 'CST', returns an 89-character (161 for Opt. 001) ASCII string of all current mode/parameter settings. Order is fixed except HIL/LOL vs OFS/AMP depending on which is active. Used to save/identify the full instrument state.

commands.name: interrogate_error
commands.description: Interrogate error. After a Service Request, returns a more detailed description of the error (e.g. duty cycle or width error when TIMING ERROR is indicated in the status byte).

commands.name: device_clear
commands.description: HP-IB Universal Command Device Clear. Loads the standard parameter set stored in 8116A ROMs. After DCL the 8116A does not remain in remote mode. Issued via the bus (e.g. CLEAR 7), not as an ASCII string.

commands.name: selected_device_clear
commands.description: HP-IB Selected Device Clear. Loads the standard parameter set stored in 8116A ROMs; the 8116A remains in remote mode. Issued via the bus (e.g. CLEAR 716).

commands.name: group_execute_trigger
commands.description: HP-IB Group Execute Trigger. When the 8116A is in trigger, external sweep, or burst mode, this bus message initiates a single cycle, sweep, or burst respectively. Issued via the bus (e.g. TRIGGER 716).
